meta.title: The effects of the polyene antibiotic mepartricin on polymorphonuclear leucocyte function: an in-vitro study -- Sacchi et al. 12 (1): 143 -- Journal of Medical Microbiology

abstractHtml: Mepartricin, a polyene antibiotic with candidacidal and trichomonicidal activity, was found to be without toxic effects for human polymorphonuclear leucocytes; the drug seems to be unable to enter the human cells. Some synergism between the antifungal activities of mepartricin and of human leucocytes is seen if Candida cells are pre-incubated with sub-lethal concentrations of the drug.
